heading: Track Performance Over Time
body:
Evaluation should not be a one-time event. Each time you change a
component in your system, evaluate the changes to assess how they
impact performance. Once your application is in production, monitor
performance in real time and detect changes.

Use [Charts](charts) to monitor the performance
of your LLM (Large Language Model) application. Write evaluation results and any feedback
metrics you want to track to an Atlas collection:

```python
from datetime import datetime

result["timestamp"] = datetime.now()
collection = db["metrics"]
collection.insert_one(result)
```

This code adds a `timestamp` field to the evaluation result and
writes it to a `metrics` collection in the `ragas_evals` database.
The document in Atlas looks like this:

```json
{
  "answer_similarity": 0.8873,
  "answer_correctness": 0.5922,
  "timestamp": "2024-04-07T23:27:30.655+00:00"
}
```

Create a dashboard in MongoDB Charts to visualize your metrics over time.
To learn how to create charts and dashboards, see
[Build Charts](charts/build-charts).
